title: drymocallis mollis
intro: Drymocallis mollis, also known as western sweetroot, is a perennial plant native to the western parts of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate skin conditions. The plant's roots have been used to create herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Additionally, the plant's roots have been used to create a sweet-tasting syrup, which has been used to treat various health issues, including diarrhea and stomach ulcers.
